Our school must be built around the curiosity of the student, not the convenience of the schedule.
A founder's notes should compound across months, not vanish into a drawer.
Every method we promote should be testable against the next cohort's outcomes.
If a conclusion cannot survive its own counterexamples, it does not belong in the canon.
The point of the codex is to remember what we already learned so we stop paying to learn it again.
